# @stll/regex-set

> NAPI-RS bindings to Rust regex-automata for
> multi-pattern regex matching in Node.js and Bun.
> Single pass, O(m*n) guaranteed, no backtracking.

## Quick start

```typescript
import { RegexSet } from "@stll/regex-set";

const rs = new RegexSet([
  "\\d{2}\\.\\d{2}\\.\\d{4}",
  "\\+?\\d{9,12}",
  "[A-Z]{2}\\d{6}",
]);

rs.findIter("Born 15.03.1990, ID CZ123456");
// [{ pattern: 0, start: 5, end: 15, text: "15.03.1990" },
//  { pattern: 2, start: 20, end: 28, text: "CZ123456" }]

rs.replaceAll(text, ["[DATE]", "[PHONE]", "[ID]"]);
```

## API

Constructor: `new RegexSet(patterns: string[])`

Patterns use Rust regex syntax (like PCRE but
no backreferences or lookaround).

Methods:
- `findIter(haystack: string): Match[]`
- `isMatch(haystack: string): boolean`
- `whichMatch(haystack: string): number[]`
- `replaceAll(haystack: string, replacements: string[]): string`
- `patternCount: number`

Match type (same as @stll/aho-corasick):
```typescript
type Match = {
  pattern: number;
  start: number;
  end: number;
  text: string;
};
```

Offsets are UTF-16 code units, compatible with
`String.prototype.slice()`.

## Important notes

- Guaranteed O(m * n). No catastrophic backtracking.
- Rust regex syntax: no backreferences, no lookaround.
- `\d`, `\w`, `\s` are Unicode-aware by default.
- Native dependency: requires prebuilt binary or Rust toolchain.
- Composable with @stll/aho-corasick (same Match type).
